title: The Best Amazon Prime Day Headphone Deals for Every Kind of Listener
gist: WIRED headphone deals guide for Prime Day covering the full range from $19 budget earbuds to $399 Apple AirPods Max 2. Key competitive insight: Sony WH-1000XM6 at $378 is the top noise-canceling pick, but Sony is simultaneously launching a $650 flagship (1000X The Collexion) which is not yet discounted. Bose QuietComfort Ultra at $269 (40% off from $449) is described as one of the steeper discounts in the article.

insights.claim: Sony's WH-1000XM6 at $378 (18% off from $460) remained WIRED's top noise-canceling headphone pick, but Sony simultaneously launched a new $650 flagship called the 1000X The Collexion that was not discounted—signaling a two-tier product strategy.
insights.speaker: Chris Haslam
insights.so_what: Sony's $650 flagship launch while the XM6 still commands full editorial recommendation suggests Sony is testing luxury pricing headroom without cannibalizing the XM6's dominant mid-premium segment position.
insights.quote: It will be a while before you see Sony's shiny new $650 1000X The Collexion discounted, but don't worry, because the on-sale Sony WH-1000XM6 remain one of the finest noise-canceling headphones ever.

insights.claim: Bose QuietComfort Ultra at $269 (40% off from $449) was available during Prime Day—the steepest absolute dollar discount in the headphone guide—despite WIRED noting the headphones 'remain one of the best buys despite being over two years old.'
insights.speaker: Chris Haslam
insights.so_what: A 40% markdown on a 2+ year old flagship headphone suggests Bose is clearing inventory ahead of a product refresh or responding to Sony XM6 competitive pressure.
insights.quote: It's a testament to the quality of the Bose QuietComfort Ultra that they remain one of the best buys despite being over two years old.

insights.claim: Google Pixel Buds Pro 2 at $161 (30% off from $229) added Gemini AI integration, head gesture control, and adaptive audio in a 2025 software update—making them WIRED's top recommendation for Android users.
insights.speaker: Chris Haslam
insights.so_what: Google's Gemini integration into Pixel Buds deepens the Android ecosystem lock-in by adding AI-powered audio features as an earbud differentiator—relevant to Google's broader wearables and AI assistant strategy.
insights.quote: Google updated the software back in 2025 to add adaptive audio, protection against loud noise, improved Gemini integration and head gesture control
